title: Phrase and clause placement
description: Choose the best placement for the phrase or clause in the sentences provided!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Combine the sentence and clause provided to create the most logical option.**

> Sentence:
> Maria likes to go scuba diving

> Clause:
> When she is on vacation

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can combine **clauses** to create complete sentences in the following ways:

* Independent clause + comma + coordinating conjunction + independent clause
* Independent clause + semicolon + independent clause
* Independent clause + dependent clause
* Dependent clause + comma + independent clause

**Phrases** can appear at the start, at the end, or in the middle of a clause.

**Most importantly, we must combine phrases and clauses in a way that makes the meaning of the sentence clear!**

The most logical combination is

>**When she is on vacation, Maria likes to go scuba diving.**

choices.content: When she is on vacation, Maria likes to go scuba diving.
Maria when she is on vacation likes to go scuba diving.
Maria likes when she is on vacation to go scuba diving.
